Since the last Elden Ring update, the game does not launch for me anymore. No matter the Proton version. I tried Experimental Bleeding Edge, 7.0-6 and 7-41. Playing on an RX 6800 XT with up to date drivers, using Mesa-nonfree from the Manjaro maintainers.

Does anybody have the same problem? The game pretty much does not launch anymore.

For me the game (and mangohud) does work on Proton 8.0 if I disable EAC, but with EAC enabled the game doesn't launch, even with mangohud disabled. Switching back to proton 7.X fixes the incompatibility with EAC for me.

**Update 2**
Created new prefix/profile with 8.0-1 and even under these circumstances the game goes past EAC and then nothing.
Seems like an issue with Proton 8.0-1 and/or the new runtime?

---
**Update 1**
Also tried Proton GE 7-49 and indeed it works - it seems that version 8 has some issues, on both Desktop and SteamDeck.
Shall I clear my wine/proton profile and let it rebuild from scratch? (happy to do as long as folks confirm games are saved on the cloud!)

Hi, it seems like it's not running neither on my desktop nor on my SteamDeck (stable OS, I'm using setup recommended by Valve there).
When I switch to Proton 7.0-6 it does run on my desktop (Ubuntu 22.04 + Nvidia 525.105.17), but if I select Proton Experimental or 8.0-1 it goes past the EAC screen then that's it.
Same behaviour on SteamDeck.

Am I the only one experiencing this?

This is the command line on the desktop:
```
ZMANGOHUD=1 MANGOHUD_CONFIG=gpu_power,gpu_temp,cpu_temp,position=top-right,alpha=0.5 %command%
```
(not the ZMANGOHUD so it's disabled)

And this is my `System Information`: https://gist.github.com/Emanem/0a507a60a2c6bb0dfd034c7bf592d0a4

> **Update 2** Created new prefix/profile with 8.0-1 and even under these circumstances the game goes past EAC and then nothing. Seems like an issue with Proton 8.0-1 and/or the new runtime?

Yes, probably.
If I put the line
`echo "%command%" | sed 's/start_protected_game.exe/eldenring.exe/' | sh`
as my launch options to disable EAC, the game works fine on Proton 8, however, obviously no multiplayer that way.
Meanwhile, launching with EAC is completely borked with Proton 8.0-1d.
Latest Proton Experimental 8.0-20230417 seems to have fixed the issue!

> Latest Proton Experimental 8.0-20230417 seems to have fixed the issue!

That's a nope for me, using _bleeding edge_ branch.

I will point out that under Proton 8 it often takes many, many attempts to get the game to launch correctly, far in excess of what is reasonable. This is almost certainly due to EAC, since launching with `echo "%command%" | sed 's/start_protected_game.exe/eldenring.exe/' | sh` to bypass it works correctly every time. Not sure if there's anything that can be done to correct the issue.

Okay I finally found the solution and the reason why Elden Ring was not launching for me anymore! The short answer is that for some reason you have to trigger Steam to reinstall the dependencies like EAC and so on in the prefix. Something during one of the last updates of the game simply broke compatibility with existing installations. I don't know any details beyond these assumptions, but here is how to fix it:

As I found on ProtonDB, someone suggested to "Backup and delete the Elden Ring (1245620) subdirectory in compatdata".

1. Backup and delete the Elden Ring (1245620) subdirectory in compatdata -> Simply cut and paste it into another folder (e.g. ~/Downloads/). I want to reiterate that this step of essentially backing up this directory is very important! **Your saves are stored in there!**
2. Start Elden Ring via Steam. It will now create a fresh prefix folder (1245620 in Steam's compatdata folder). It will also install all dependencies like EAC, etc.
3. Now you have essentially a fresh install of Elden Ring with no save games. The game will already launch fine. Now we just need to restore your saves from the backed up folder!
4. Your backed up folder (that you pasted somewhere else) is called "1245620". Now navigate to "1245620/pfx/drive_c/users/steamuser/AppData/Roaming/EldenRing/" and navigate to this same location in the "1245620" folder that was just re-created by Steam in the compatdata folder. After this step, you should have both "EldenRing" folders open side by side. From your backed up folder, simply copy the folder containing your save games as well as the "GraphicsConfig.xml" into the other corresponding folder used by Steam. Now you have all of your save games as they were before.
5. Start the game and you will be able to continue from your saves as expected. You are now good to go! Enjoy!

> for some reason you have to trigger Steam to reinstall the dependencies like EAC and so on in the prefix.

That unfortunately does not work for me. EAC ist still borked on Latest Proton Experimental Bleeding Edge, without EAC it works fine.

Elden Ring and/or Proton doesn't seem to like the new Steam Overlay, and seems to crash consistently after staying in a black screen for a while at least with Proton 8. I found two workarounds, though:

* Disable Steam Overlay (obviously). This isn't preferable though.
* Launch the game. When the game window appears but before the game crashes, switch out of the desktop environment to TTY2 (or something else) using `Ctrl + Alt + F2`. Once you hear sound (meaning the game has launched and reached the main menu), switch back to the desktop environment with `Ctrl + Alt + F1`. The game and Steam Overlay should now work fine. The exact TTY numbers might depend on your system. On my Arch system the desktop environment is under TTY1, but supposedly it could be TTY2 on other systems.

This is with the following system:

* Arch Linux running KDE Plasma on X11.
* Steam Beta, version: 1682723851
* AMD Radeon 6900XT on Mesa 23.0.3-1

EDIT: Forgot to mention that I'm also launching the game using [er-patcher](https://github.com/gurrgur/er-patcher) to bypass the 60 FPS lock. In practice, this launches eldenring.exe instead of start_protected_game.exe with the following Steam launch options:

```
python3 er-patcher --rate 144 -- %command%
```

I'll look into creating a Proton log shortly. The `PROTON_LOG=1` with this command seems to cause the game to stay at the black screen with one CPU core at 100% while the log file continues to grow (it went as far as 40 GB before I closed the game). I'll try to look into this further later.

EDIT 2:

Now I'm having trouble reproducing the issue, since the game now seems to launch perfectly each time with the aforementioned command that originally crashed consistently. Some sort of race condition?

Hello @Matoking, please add [`PROTON_LOG=1 %command%`](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton#runtime-config-options) to the game's launch options, reproduce the issue, and attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file. (Proton logs compress well if needed.) Also, please temporarily opt out of Steam's client beta and copy your system information from Steam (`Steam` -> `Help` -> `System Information`) and put it in a [gist](https://gist.github.com/), then include a link to the gist in this issue report.

With RT off, all is well, ignoring the fact that the game refuses to work in online mode.

Testing with Proton 8.0-2 and experimental (8.0-2c). I noticed no difference.

* CPU: Ryzen 5 3600
* GPU: RX 6600 XT
* OS: Devuan chimaera (with some packages from daedalus to allow compilation of recent Mesa)

VKD3D_CONFIG=dxr,dxr11
PROTON_CONFIG=dxr,dxr11
VKD3D_FEATURE_LEVEL=12_1

Can someone tell me how to fix it.

Elden Ring stops handling input from the Steam Deck's integrated controller on the SteamOS 3.5 from the main branch and the latest Steam beta client. An external xinput gamepad connected via bluetooth continues to work once the game gets into this bad state.

The controller doesn't appear to have its firmware in a bad state either. It's fully functional in the OS. The d-pad, the ABXY buttons, triggers, STEAM button, QAM button, other buttons and the joysticks are all working properly in the OS.

Getting it out of this state involves force closing the game and starting it again. It doesn't take down the OS with it in this failure mode. The dmesg output doesn't show anything particularly interesting. It has messages related to LUTs and color profile (debug messages containing "Josh color"). Perhaps these were part of the ongoing work with color profiles.

The game also responds to keyboard input, touch screen input and touchpad as mouse input while it is in the bad state.

I've been able to reproduce the issue with another device running Windows with an xinput controller. I had to close the game and start it again on that other device. The other device was on the stable version of Steam.

The fact that two different devices running different operating systems with different hardware had the same issue rules out a few potential culprits: Proton, SteamOS, my Steam Deck, the controllers themselves and Windows. The game was also not updated recently. That leaves us only with a bug in Steam input.

As an additional bonus, Steam input didn't set up my xinput controller properly. Is there some broken profile applied incorrectly? It's odd because it used to work just fine. It always has.

I've written this in case it helps track down a bug.

**Your system information**

    Steam client version (build number or date): 28.04.2023
    Distribution: Slackware Current (post 15.0)
    Opted into Steam client beta?: Yes
    Have you checked for system updates?: Yes

**Please describe your issue in as much detail as possible:**

Elden Ring will not launch with any version of Proton except 7.3-GE-1. It shows a launch window with the EAC logo in the bottom left, a progress bar goes up to 100%, and then:

![image](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/assets/47920497/af48e4b3-b7ba-45a4-9a5e-ffecacf6f43e)

Using 7.3-GE-1 or the workaround described in https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/5613#issuecomment-105052494 allows me to load into the game, but I get the "Inappropriate activity messages" shown in previous comments and online play is disabled. For the workaround, I actually see the online menu for a moment before I get this. It seems to log in, THEN detect inappropriate activity, then log me out, whereas with 7.3-GE-1 I'm just logged out.

I have both PROTON_LOG=1 and VKD3D_FEATURE_LEVEL=12_0 enabled in launch options. The latter was advised by a thread on Reddit but I'm not sure it changed anything.

[Log with Proton Experimental](https://gist.github.com/MurdoMaclachlan/14353eb671466624e1229313d957873b)

[Log with 7.3-GE-1](https://gist.github.com/MurdoMaclachlan/3cc6f7a3109ff3a75071d0498d6cb6b3)

I've yet to test out other recording software to see but I'm pretty confident its a Proton issue.

My specs: 5600x, X570A Pro MSI,1080 Ti, 16GB 3200mhz
Desktop Env/OS: Linux Mint 21.2, X11/Xorg, Cinnamon, Gdm3

@curbthepain

Have nigh the exact same specs. Unfortunately there is unlikely to be any improvement in performance, Pascal GPUs are missing key features (Bindless Uniform Buffers being the notable one) for VKD3D to function performantly in some cases. This is unlikely to ever be feasible to work around.

I have absolutely no clue why this might be happening. Any help is greatly appreciated.

`Assertion failed: !status && "vkGetPhysicalDeviceSurfaceCapabilitiesKHR", file ../src-wine/dlls/winevulkan/loader_thunks.c, line 4912`

in terminal and quits before any window opens.

Currently on Hyprland with linux-zen 6.6.7.zen1, PRIME setup with Intel UHD 630 as iGPU and NVIDIA 1660Ti as dGPU.

Hello @LazarusCat59, `warn:seh:handle_syscall_fault backtrace: --- Exception 0xc0000005 at 0xf63d8c2f: /usr/lib/pressure-vessel/overrides/lib/i386-linux-gnu/libnvidia-glcore.so.545.29.06 + 0xdd8c2f.` in your Proton log hints towards an access violation (c0000005) in your video driver which should also be mentioned to your video driver vendor.

This reminds me of a comment at https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/150#issuecomment-1848610458, but it's unlikely that workaround would help based on your system information.

> Replying to https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/5613#issuecomment-1862843664

Just tried out the workaround mentioned in linked comment and nope it does not help indeed. Will try reporting to NVIDIA about this.

1. You can find the Steam AppID in the URL of the shop page of the game.
   e.g. for `The Witcher 3: Wild Hunt` the AppID is `292030`.
2. You can find your driver and Linux version, as well as your graphics
   processor's name in the system information report of Steam.

4. Please copy it to your clipboard by pressing `Ctrl+A` and then `Ctrl+C`.

5. Also, please copy the contents of `Help` > `Steam Runtime Information` to
   the gist.
6. Please search for open issues and pull requests by the name of the game and
   find out whether they are relevant and should be referenced above.
-->

compatibility_report:
# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Hyperdimension Neptunia Re;Birth2 Sisters Generation
- Steam AppID of the game: 351710

## System Information
- GPU: RX 480
- Driver/LLVM version: Mesa 20.3.2
- Kernel version: 5.10.6-110-tkg-bmq
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/):https://gist.github.com/CygnusTerminal/70e276f032863ea0f3f6337f7ef31b03
- Proton version: 5.0-10/5.13-4

## I confirm:
- [x] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [x] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and drag
and drop the generated `$HOME/steam-$APPID.log` into this issue report -->
[steam-351710.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/5820461/steam-351710.log)


## Symptoms <!-- What's the problem? -->
Since days ago, this game and the other two (Re;birth 1 and 3) have been running pretty bad on my machine, fights and dungeons are below the 20 FPS mark. In combat, when the characters transform, the FPS go up to my vsync limit, only to go back to 17~20 when it's over. They use OpenGL. Native OpenGL games work like a charm (tested the entire Half-Life 2 franchise and Left 4 Dead franchise). I could observe the GPU usage ranging from values between 0% and 90%, every second. I've tried clean prefixes, disabling compositor manually, gamemode, no gamemode, setting the governor to performance, closing all background apps. Nothing seems to work. On Windows the games work as intended. I have also recorded a video with my external capture card, so the issues can be understood better (https://youtu.be/0JaVWKphLz0). Also, aside from the low quality due to pulseaudio being pulseaudio, and ALSA sounding with enough reverb to blow my eardrums, the popping is very noticiable, this has been an issue i've benn having with Wine/Proton since a month ago.

## Reproduction
Start the game, enter a dungeon/fight/observe the performance.

following_discussion.discussion_body:
Hello @CygnusTerminal, in general, we're using one issue report per unofficially supported game title, and since both games in #189 are equally affected, I've gone ahead and transferred this issue report to https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/189#issuecomment-760220210.

If at some point there's an indication that there's a difference in the behavior between those two games, then it would make sense to track them separately.

the demo is playable for everyone

https://discord.gg/yfswCNxKCu

## System Information
- Operating System Version: "Manjaro Linux" (64 bit)
- GPU: NVIDIA Corporation NVIDIA GeForce GTX 1060 6GB/PCIe/SSE2
- Video driver version: 4.6.0 NVIDIA 535.104.05
- Kernel version: 6.5.5-2-cachyos
- Link to full system information report: https://gist.github.com/monarc99/10e84b4f7db886bc5712b4f2f0dca197

[bugreport-vkd3d-2552960.log](https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/files/12792632/bugreport-vkd3d-2552960.log)

## Symptoms 

the report is for the playable demo - the full game will be released in the future.

proton 8.0-3: 

the demo starts and is playable on my system. There are minor graphic bugs. If you want to build a road, some graphic elements are missing.

normally it looks like this in windows 11:
https://pioneers-wiki.com/wp-content/uploads/2023/09/schnelltest-768x213.webp

with proton you must build the roads and buildings blind. 

But the demo is fully playable on my system. I played the full demo (7 hours)
Some other linux players report a crash, if they build something - even with proton 8.0-3 


proton 8.0-4-rc or any newer proton: regression

the game starts with a error message. If you minimize the error message and open a new map, the game crash, if you place something on the map.

error message at start:

compatibility_report:
Satisfactory - mouse over text input box teleports mouse cursor

Issue transferred from https://github.com/ValveSoftware/Proton/issues/8165.
@Omnifarious posted on 2024-10-15T19:51:25:

# Compatibility Report
- Name of the game with compatibility issues: Satisfactory
- Steam AppID of the game: 526870

## System Information
- GPU: ATI Radeon 7900 XTX
- Video driver version: Mesa 24.2.4
- Kernel version: 6.11.2-300.fc41.x86_64
- Link to full system information report as [Gist](https://gist.github.com/): https://gist.github.com/Omnifarious/8ac4447b69bc9c99c1607ab71727a5a4
- Proton version: Experimental (as of Oct 15, 2024)

I will replace the above bit with my system information from the affected system.

## I confirm:
- [X] that I haven't found an existing compatibility report for this game.
- [X] that I have checked whether there are updates for my system available.

<!-- Please add `PROTON_LOG=1 %command%` to the game's launch options and
attach the generated $HOME/steam-$APPID.log to this issue report as a file.
I will do this.

## Symptoms
Whenever I mouse over a text input box in the main game, my mouse teleports away from the box.


## Reproduction
Start the game, and then start a new game or load an existing game.
Press 'Q' (the default keybind for the build tool) to bring up a dialogue box of stuff to build.
Go over to the "search for building" box
See your mouse cursor teleport away

Oddly, this works on the system I detail here, which is why I think it's a Proton issue. I suspect this may have something to do with Fedora 41 and the fact it uses Wayland by default. It looks like the game is trying to switch to the system "I-bar" cursor for text entry when moving over a text entry box. And I suspect that mishandling this situation is what's causing the problem.

I should check to see if maybe FSR is causing the problem by getting some part of the system confused about screen space coordinates. Checking that, no effect. And the teleport seems to happen to a fixed location, which wouldn't be the case if it was coordinate confusion.

Link to gist of system that functions correctly: https://gist.github.com/Omnifarious/c521ef6a30543b25eaf20652878a0e1c

following_discussion.discussion_body:
Upon exiting Satisfactory  "1.0" i get an error (both on this official Proton variant as well as the GE variant.)
![image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7baf1672-6529-4dd7-ad34-53a5bb778e46)

This wasn't an issue in Satisfactory update8
The game works pretty much flawlessly for me, except that same error message upon closing the game.

After upgrading from Fedora 39 to Fedora 41 issue with mouse cursor jumping from input fields and when grabbing some controls that should be dragged, started happening. Proton 9.0-3. GNOME 47, Wayland.

The issue is definitely related to remote play.

I'm running Arch Linux  with NVIDIA 1070 GPU and was trying to stream to my M2 MacBook Pro. 

Game ran fine on Arch but after attempting to stream to the MacBook, the game wouldn't launch anymore.

I run it from Steam, Proton 9.0-3 and `gamemoderun %command%`

After attempting to stream, I had to run this to make it work on Arch again.
`
 rm -rf ~/.steam/steam/steamapps/compatdata/526870/`
Same as other here, game works fine but I get vkDestroySampler error on exit

> Satisfactory - mouse over text input box teleports mouse cursor
 
I'm having the same issue using pop_os up to date as of 2024-11-23, using a rx-6900xt and the kernel's reporting as `	Linux 6.9.3-76060903-generic (x86_64)`

My work-around is that if I plug in an xbox controller I can use the right joystick to move the cursor over the few text boxes I need and I can use the mouse to click from there.

> Satisfactory - mouse over text input box teleports mouse cursor
> 

I have the same issue with Wayland, but not with XOrg. 

Does anyone have workaround suggestions?

@Ruedii Not seeing that here with gnome/plasma wayland with proton 9. At least with the text input fields that you can find in the main menu from the new game and join game submenus, as well as the one in the in-game's builder menu. Does it only happen with specific text input fields? with what desktop environment?

I'm seeing an issue where playing from a Steam Deck in the official Dock causes audio to always come from the deck's speakers, disregarding the "use external sound" Steam system setting. The only way I was able to work around this was by booting into desktop mode, launching Satisfactory, then setting its OS-level application-specific audio output to be the external source (whose name might vary, depending on what you're plugging the dock into? can't tell). From that point on, including after I rebooted back into the regular Deck UI, it used the correct output. I didn't see any options in-game to set the audio output (and that doesn't seem like a usual feature) - so I'm not sure exactly whether the root of the problem was in the game, in Proton, or somewhere else.

There have been updates to Satisfactory since I last experienced the
issue.  I will have to give it another go next time I am experimenting with
such stuff.

For replication's sake:  My Wayland environment is KWin/Wayland with
Plasma.  I have a mouse, and no touchscreen.  I do not have GNOME
compatibility installed, but do have basic GTK+ compatibility installed.

It may be environment specific.

Notably KWin/Wayland uses a scaler compositor on full screen programs, and
passes through relative inputs such as mice in raw format.  This may be
related.

> Message ID: ***@***.***>
>

>> Satisfactory - mouse over text input box teleports mouse cursor

> Does anyone have workaround suggestions?

@Ruedii I don't know why this works but setting the launch command as such: `gamemoderun gamescope --fullscreen --force-grab-cursor --backend wayland -H 2160 -h 2160 -- %command%` seems to fix the teleporting mouse cursor for me.

EDIT: Change the resolution to whatever you're using (`2160` for me above).

I'm having an issue with the map in satisfactory. Supposedly you're able to scroll the map around by clicking and dragging with right click. I've never been able to get this to work, though I haven't tried if it's possible on Windows. Does anyone else have this working?

> I'm having an issue with the map in satisfactory. Supposedly you're able to scroll the map around by clicking and dragging with right click. I've never been able to get this to work, though I haven't tried if it's possible on Windows. Does anyone else have this working?

Never been a problem for me. Though I can't remember if it is right or left click - it's all in my muscle memory.
